detail:
WMI-3 attacker content in authoritative position (offset-controlled-write): external content is written at an attacker-controlled offset ('vaddr') into object 'struct iosys_map' — reaching past its buffer into a trailing field (the authoritative position the consumer follows). \[mechanism:write-at-attacker-offset;indicates=WMI-3\] \[mem:struct=iosys_map;field=vaddr;offset=?\] \[oracle:assert=vaddr stays within the object's declared buffer;assume=external content written at a variable offset into the object\]
WMI-3 attacker content in authoritative position (offset-controlled-write): external content is written at an attacker-controlled offset ('dst_offset') into object 'struct iosys_map' — reaching past its buffer into a trailing field (the authoritative position the consumer follows). \[mechanism:write-at-attacker-offset;indicates=WMI-3\] \[mem:struct=iosys_map;field=dst_offset;offset=?\] \[oracle:assert=dst_offset stays within the object's declared buffer;assume=external content written at a variable offset into the object\]
